$schema_note: B1 retrieval-quality eval baseline (Task 7). Consumed by eval/rag/harness/verdict.ts via the BaselineConfig shape: { metrics, bands }. The `metrics` values are the per-metric baseline on the CURRENT production retrieval settings (RRF k=60, MMR lambda=0.7, fetch-N=40, floor=0.5 via retrieve()). The `bands` are the A7 per-metric regress bands (spec section B1.5).
settings_note: Baseline measured on production settings: RRF k=60, MMR lambda=0.7, fetch-N=40, floor=0.5 via retrieve(). A baseline refresh is an explicit, reviewed action (re-run on current settings + commit) — NEVER automatic, so the gate cannot silently rebaseline a regression away.
bands_note: A7 per-metric regress bands (spec section B1.5). RELATIVE bands trip when drop > band*baseline; ABSOLUTE bands are percentage points (2pp = 0.02) and trip when drop > band. These bands are ASSESSMENT-REVIEWED and NEVER AUTO-REFRESHED. Changing any band value requires assessment review on the baseline file. The band STRUCTURE ships in Task 7; the metric VALUES are populated by a reviewed full-path run in Task 9/10.
placeholder_note: TODO(Task 10): the `metrics` values below are PLACEHOLDERS — they are NOT a real measurement. Task 10 runs the harness full-path (VOYAGE_API_KEY present) on the Task 9 golden set against current production settings and commits the resulting metric values here (a reviewed action). Until then these values MUST NOT be used to gate any tuning decision. The `bands` block is real and ships in Task 7.
